name: Watermelon Fruit Basket
recipeIngredient: 1 medium (5 cups) pineapple, cut into chunks
1 large (4 cups) honeydew or cantaloupe melon, cut into chunks or balls
1 pint (2 cups) strawberries, blueberries, or raspberries
1 lb (2 cups) green or red grapes
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a thin slice from the bottom of the watermelon so it will sit flat, being careful not to cut through the rind.
2. With a pencil and a 1-1/2 inch wide cardboard guide, draw a scallop design about one-third of the way down from the top of the melon.
3. On the top third mark, place the handle.
4. Carefully cut the watermelon to create scallops and the handle using a sharp paring knife.
5. Remove the top sections.
6. Scoop the melon into balls or wedges, reserving 6 to 8 cups for the fruit basket. (Refrigerate the remaining melon for other uses)
7. Leave the shell about one inch thick.
8. Wrap the watermelon basket in plastic wrap and refrigerate until ready to serve.
9. Fill with fresh fruit just before serving.
10. Makes approximately 42 half-cup servings (about 30 calories per serving)
TIPS:
Vary the fruit shapes and colors for an attractive salad. Include balls, spears, cubes, and triangles.
To use the most melon pulp, cut chunks or cubes rather than melon balls.
Use grapes, cherries, and strawberries for round shapes.
Blueberries and raspberries are best used as a garnish since they are fragile and bruise easily when tossed with other fruits.
Sprinkle bananas, apples, and peaches with orange or lemon juice to prevent discoloration.
